description: Synthesis pack: cross-paper connections among four 2025-2026 interpretability results — global workspace in LLMs (transformer-circuits 2026), psychological steering (arXiv:2510.04484), KV cache redundancy (arXiv:2603.19664), and latent introspection (arXiv:2602.20031). Captures the emerging picture: the residual stream as the substrate, a verbalizable workspace as the privileged subspace, and injection/steering/introspection as operations on it.
source: Synthesis across four primary sources, extracted 2026-07-06. See companion packs: global-workspace-theory, psychological-steering, residual-stream-kv, latent-introspection.

triples.subject: residual stream
triples.object: All four papers treat the residual stream as the fundamental substrate: the workspace paper reads verbalizable concepts from mid-layer residual activations (J-space); the KV paper proves the residual stream is the sufficient statistic for inference (keys/values are derived projections); the introspection paper finds injection-detection signals in the residual stream via logit lens; steering papers write emotion/personality vectors directly into it.

triples.subject: workspace vs latent signals
triples.object: The workspace paper says only what enters J-space is verbally reportable; the latent introspection paper shows detection signals that exist in the residual stream but fade before final layers and go unreported (0.3% baseline sensitivity). Together: information outside the workspace is real but silent, and elicitation (introspection-informed prompting, 0.3% -> 39.9%) works by moving latent content into the reportable workspace.

triples.subject: concept injection
triples.object: Psychological steering studies injection as control (how well can we write states in, and at what trustworthiness cost); latent introspection studies injection as detection (can the model notice it was written to). The same intervention class — adding vectors to activations — defines both an attack/control surface and a self-monitoring test.

triples.subject: directed modulation and introspection
triples.object: The workspace paper shows models can deliberately activate concepts in J-space (top-down modulation); the introspection paper shows models can recover injected concepts when properly prompted (1.05 bits mutual information). Both indicate models have functional read/write access to their own intermediate representations.

triples.subject: self-report reliability
triples.object: Model self-reports are systematically incomplete rather than meaningless: J-space ablation abolishes experiential language (report tracks a real internal structure), yet default denials of concept injection are false negatives (real internal signals go unreported). Self-report is evidence about workspace contents, not about the whole model state.

triples.subject: steering trustworthiness and workspace
triples.object: Steering side effects (joy degrading adversarial factuality, anger raising toxicity but hardening leakage resistance) are consistent with the workspace being small (6-10% of variance) and broadcast-coupled: writing an affect vector into a shared broadcast space perturbs many downstream consumers at once, so state induction cannot be surgically isolated from safety-relevant behavior.

triples.subject: KV redundancy and activation-space methods
triples.object: If per-token state reduces losslessly to residual vectors (5 KB vs 136 KB, bit-identical outputs), then cache-level techniques — knowledge injection into the KV cache, steering, workspace readout — can all be reformulated as operations on stored residual streams, with keys/values recomputed on demand (up to 5x faster than cache reads at moderate batch sizes).

triples.subject: global workspace theory in LLMs
triples.object: The workspace paper provides functional (not phenomenal) evidence for access-consciousness-like structure in LLMs; the introspection paper adds functional introspective access to internal states. Both groups explicitly decline to draw phenomenal conclusions, but jointly they move machine-consciousness questions from philosophy into measurable mechanism.

triples.subject: elicitation gap
triples.object: A recurring 2026 result: capabilities measured behaviorally at near zero can be latent and unlockable — introspective detection jumps >100x with accurate mechanistic framing; workspace contents causally drive reasoning (54-70% output change on vector swaps) despite being a small variance fraction. Behavioral nulls are weak evidence of mechanistic absence.

triples.subject: safety agenda implied by these papers
triples.object: (1) Monitor the residual stream, not just outputs — that is where both state and its manipulation live; (2) treat persona/affect steering as a safety surface with measurable trustworthiness costs; (3) do not trust denial-of-manipulation self-reports without workspace-level checks; (4) lossless residual-stream checkpointing makes such monitoring cheaper than full KV retention.
